title: Emily Henry Tells Jenna She Asked to ‘Take a Step Back’ From Movie Adaptations After ‘Harrowing’ Experience
summary: Emily Henry said she had to take a step back from adaptations of her movies after experience on ‘People We Meet on Vacation.’
story: Emily Henry’s novels are sweeping Hollywood. Now, she’s deciding the role she wants to have in the process.
In the Season 3 premiere episode of Jenna Bush Hager’s podcast, “Open Book with Jenna” on June 11, Henry opened up about the process of watching her rom-coms hit the screen.

The first of five planned adaptations, “People We Meet on Vacation,” hit Netflix in January. Four other books by Henry are also headed for the screen: “Beach Read,” “Book Lovers,” “Happy Place” and “Funny Story.”

Henry is writing the screenplays for “Funny Story” and “Happy Place,” but not “Book Lovers” and “Beach Read.” The script for “People We Meet on Vacation” was written by Yulin Kuang, who’s also set to write and direct “Beach Read.”
Listen to the new episode of “Open Book with Jenna.”
While speaking to Jenna, Henry explained that “People We Meet on Vacation” was a learning experience for her.

“It’s funny being behind the scenes. It’s kind of a harrowing experience in a way, because there are so many steps before it gets to that point, and you read so many different versions of the draft,” she said.
The process was emotional, she said. “You’re trying not to get your hopes up, but you’re getting hopes up,” she said.
That’s when she decided to remove herself from more of the minute updates from set.

“I had developed a really close friendship with our director (Brett Haley), and so he was the one keeping me in the loop about everything. Toward the end of the process, I did tell him, like, ‘I actually have to step back. This feels like so many false starts and so emotional,’” she said.
Moving forward, Henry said she’s taking a more hands-off approach to adaptations of her books she’s not directly involved in.
“It can be really hard to know every single thing that’s going on and all the ups and downs,” Henry said.
“Especially when you have no control over it,” Jenna added.
“Yeah, exactly,” Henry said.
Writing “Funny Story” and “Happy Place” has let her hold onto the books “a little bit longer.”
But it “still will not be just my thing,” she said. “It’s going belong to all these people. Hundreds of people are going to work on these things.”
While on set for “People We Meet on Vacation,” Henry said she tried to use “every moment” with decision-makers to “advocate for the readers.”
The director asked for a list of must-have scenes and quotes, and she said Haley “tried to fit in as much as he possibly could.”
Henry did make her preferences known, too. For example, at one point, she fought to keep in the scene where Poppy has a pregnancy scare in Tuscany, which ultimately made the movie.

“That was really important to me. ... I was so much more like over everyone’s shoulder with this one, until I was like — this is not, this is not helpful for anyone,” she said. “Especially for myself.”
She emphasized that screen adaptations are the product of hundreds of people, not just her.
“Ultimately, it’s not my thing. I’m not in the room,” she said.
In that way, she said, she’s in the same boat as her readers.

“I am with them in this. We are going to have this experience together. Of course, it’s scary to see something you love take a new form, but it’s exciting,” she said. “You have the opportunity to be surprised.”

title: Emily Henry Gives Long-Awaited Update On Her Future Book Plans
summary: Emily Henry told Jenna Bush Hager some details about her anticipated next book including the movie that inspired it and when she expects for it to come out.
story: For the first time since 2020’s “Beach Read,” romance author Emily Henry did not release a new book this year.
But book lovers, never fear: She’s working on her next book and planning to release it in the summer of 2027.

“Taking a year off was really interesting, because getting back into writing my next book, which should be out next summer, it was really — I was shocked how nervous I felt,” Henry told Jenna Bush Hager on the June 11 episode of the TODAY co-host’s “Open Book” podcast. “I felt so scared to get back into it.”
Now back into the “writing cave,” as she calls it, Henry is sparing with further details, calling the yet-to-be-announced next title a “very traditional romantic comedy.”
“Oh, I know what I can say,” Henry said. “It is not a holiday book, but it kind of feels a little bit like my take on ‘The Holiday’ in a very small way,” she tells Jenna.
Listen to the new episode of “Open Book with Jenna.”

The 2006 rom-com “The Holiday” famously follows two women, played by Cameron Diaz and Kate Winslet, who switch homes for Christmas, each finding love — with Jude Law and Jack Black, respectively — in brand new settings.

Henry is no stranger to drawing inspiration from beloved films. Her 2021 novel “People We Meet on Vacation” is an homage to “When Harry Met Sally ...,” and she told Jenna that “Book Lovers”  was “my take on ‘You’ve Got Mail,’” even if no one else picked up on it.
“No one else will see it,” Henry said of her next book’s “Holiday” inspiration. “It’s like, I can feel it.”
She also called her next book, “the book that you would write after taking a year off.”
“And it’s very much that. It might make everyone want to take a year off,” Henry said. “The person needed a break and gets a break.”

As for future stories, Henry said she wants to continue alternating between “straightforward” rom-coms and stories that “side step” the genre. This pattern started with “A Great Big Beautiful Life,” which she’s long described as “something a little bit different.”

“For this one, this is another one that’s a pretty straightforward romantic comedy,” Henry said. “My hope is that for my next book after that, I’ll kind of explore something else a little bit. Still keep it romance, but I’m kind of trying to alternate between exploring new territory and just like serving the readers what I think they’ve come to expect.”

Henry is the author of nine books — including the smash hits “Beach Read,” “People We Meet on Vacation,” “Book Lovers,” “Happy Place,” “Funny Story” and most recently, “A Great Big Beautiful Life,” almost all of which are set for screen adaptations.
Before her yearlong hiatus, which started after the publication of “A Great Big Beautiful Life” in April 2025, Henry recalled feeling “afraid to get off that carousel.”
“I do ultimately think it was really good for me to just go live my life and have new things to say, new things to write about, but it also is kind of scary,” she said.
She’s currently editing her next novel — “pretty deep, immersive editing.”
“It feels like you’re in this little tunnel and you can’t really see anything beyond your little fire pit and what it catches,” she said.
“It sounds probably terrible to a lot of people, but for a lot of writers this is like the happiest time of your year, is when you are kind of alone with your little world,” she added.

title: Kaley Cuoco Announces She’s Pregnant With Baby No. 2 With the Help of Her Family
summary: Kaley Cuoco will soon have a family of four! The actor announced that she and partner Tom Pelphrey are having a second child.
story: Kaley Cuoco will soon have a family of four!

The 40-year-old actor announced on her Instagram Wednesday, June 10, that she is currently expecting her second child with partner and fellow actor Tom Pelphrey, 43. They are already proud parents to 3-year-old daughter Matilda.

To celebrate the baby news, Cuoco posted a series of photos and videos, including one that showed her, Pelphrey and little Matilda smiling behind a cake that read “It’s a…” A chunk of the cake revealed a pink interior, confirming the two are adding another daughter to their family.

In other snaps and clips, the “Vanished” star revealed her growing baby bump. Pelphrey sweetly cradled her bump in one picture while Matilda stood nearby. Cuoco also shared a clip of the “Task” star staring in amazement at an ultrasound clip of their baby girl seemingly taken during a hospital visit. The final photo was a close-up of a sonogram where their daughter’s tiny hand is visible.
“Completing our little family,” Cuoco wrote in the caption. “What a dream come true!”
She continued, “This second time around journey has been a little windier in many ways , but wow we are so grateful for this moment!! Little sissy on the way!!!”
Cuoco referred to Pelphrey as a “girl dad for life” and added a pink bow emoji.
She ended the caption by joking that the last snap in the post “is our dainty princess giving us the middle finger!”
Celebrity friends like Alyssa Milano, Beth Behrs and Ashley Benson congratulated the couple in the comments.
Cuoco also posted the news on her Instagram story, set to Justin Bieber’s “Everything Hallelujah.”

On his page, Pelphrey shared similar snaps and wrote, “Deep gratitude and joy - so much respect and admiration for @kaleycuoco on this journey of ups and downs,” along with a series of raised-hands emoji.
“(Doc gave her a little prod for the pic and this was her immediate response…. definitely my child),” he quipped about the sonogram and added a crying-laughing emoji.
The actors, who have been together since 2022, announced on March 30, 2023 that they had welcomed their first child.
“Introducing, Matilda Carmine Richie Pelphrey, the new light of our lives!” Cuoco cheered at the time on Instagram.
She also wrote, in part, “We are blessed beyond belief @tommypelphrey didn’t think I could fall even more in love with you , but I did.”
Pelphrey called Matilda a “miracle” in his caption and said, in part, “Eternally grateful for the strength and bravery of my soulmate and best friend @kaleycuoco. You are incredible.”
Cuoco, who has since shared plenty of adorable videos of Matilda, revealed to TODAY.com that she and Pelphrey chose their oldest daughter’s name on one of their first dates.

“We looked at each other and it was like, ‘Would it be crazy to say if we had a baby we’d name her Matilda?’” she recalled. “You know when you first start dating and you’re so obsessed, and you’re planning your future children. It was that sort of thing.”
Although they decided on a name early on, she said the two kept it a secret until Matilda arrived.
“And it’s so her. She’s such a Matilda,” she said.

title: Weston McKennie’s Earliest World Cup Memory Is Set at a Fast Food Restaurant
summary: Team USA soccer star Weston McKennie talks to TODAY.com about what he’s most looking forward to as he heads to the 2026 FIFA World Cup and more.
story: Weston McKennie describes this season of his life as “challenging and maturing,” focusing on “growing day to day and year to year” on and off the pitch.
“Just bettering my game and bettering my life outside. Trying to stay true to my values and whatnot,” the 27-year-old midfielder tells TODAY.com ahead of the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

It’s a major moment in the athlete’s life as he and the U.S. Men’s National Soccer Team compete in the global soccer tournament, hosted by the United States, Mexico and Canada. The World Cup kicks off June 11, but the national team will have its first game on June 12 against Paraguay in Los Angeles, which is expected to be an emotional and pride-filled moment for McKennie.

The Puma athlete is excited for the first game on home soil, particularly hearing the national anthem and rocking his colorful football boots. The partnership with the sportswear brand, he says, was “a match that was meant to be.” He attributes it to Puma’s support off the field, including to his nonprofit, Magical Youth Mission, which supports foster children in the U.S. and kids in orphanages across Italy.
“I have a gala that I’m holding for the foundation, so they’re going to be a part of that,” McKennie says, adding it’s “the next big thing that will be coming up, besides the obvious, the World Cup.”
Below, in his own words, McKennie shares his World Cup memories, as well as what he’s most looking forward to as the tournament kicks off.
My first memory of a World Cup, I was in Germany in 2006. I was living there because my dad was in the military and I remember I was in the city of Kaiserslautern, walking around.

I was going to McDonald’s, and I remember seeing a Michael Ballack poster. And then obviously that World Cup had many events. It had the famous Zinedine Zidane headbutt against Italy and then you have Italy winning the World Cup. (But) probably my first memory: Walking into McDonald’s and seeing the Michael Ballack poster.

My biggest supporter would be my family in general and my mom, (Tina McKennie). My mom is someone that’s been with me from the beginning, traveling to all my games, going on trips with me, driving me from American football to soccer training, to American football games to soccer games, all on the same day.

I have her to thank for how my career has gone and getting me into the position to have a successful career and follow my dreams. As a parent, in the beginning stages, you always want your kid to just have fun and enjoy what they’re doing and be passionate about it, and my mom was there for that.

My dad (John McKennie) was someone that would always tell me, “If you’re not having fun doing it, then there’s no point in doing it, but if you start something, then you’re not quitting until you finish it.” I kind of had a group balance between both of my parents. My brother and sister as well, always supporting me and being there.

I feel like my biggest opponent would probably just be myself. I don’t see other people as my biggest opponent because I know what I’m capable of. I’ve had people in my life that may not be a direct opponent in the sport, but maybe an opponent (who) doesn’t want me to succeed or doesn’t believe in me, or something like that.

As long as my head is right, I know what I’m doing and I enjoy what I’m doing, and I’m focused and believe in what I’m doing, that’s great and I’ll be good. But once I start overthinking things, going back and forth in my head and caring what other people think, (that’s my biggest opponent.)

The first game, hearing the national anthem. The first time I ever heard the national anthem, I was in Portugal whenever I was with the first team of the national team. Hearing that for the first time on the professional and senior team level was amazing, and then hearing it again in the last World Cup.
Being able to hear it again on home soil with home fans and hopefully everyone’s singing it, I think it’ll be a special moment.

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Cast: Who’s Still in the Villa
summary: See the full “Love Island USA” Season 8 cast, including the eliminated islanders and new bombshells. Plus, find out who is still in the villa.
story: The first week of “Love Island USA” Season 8 is almost complete and one original islander has already been sent home.

The hit Peacock reality series returned Tuesday, June 2, and introduced 10 islanders who met and moved into the Fijian villa. First impressions were made, couples were formed and two bombshells made their mark.
After just 24 hours, the bombshells broke up some pairs, leading to some tears and one islander feeling blindsided.

But the surprises weren’t over. The bombshells then selected two islanders each and whisked them away to private beach huts. When they returned to the main villa, they announced who they wanted to couple up with, revealing their choice with a kiss.

Next, another bombshell surprised the ladies with a secret entrance during the first challenge. Tensions arose, more tears were shed and some islanders scrambled as the first recoupling ceremony of the season arrived.

Want to find out who is still in the villa? Read on for the latest villa cast updates and check back here after every episode for information about the newest bombshells. (New episodes will arrive every day except Wednesday at the same time.)
The Original Cast of ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8
Aniya Harvey
Current city: Tyrone, Georgia
Age: 23
Occupation: Marketing
Instagram: aniyaharvey02
Aniya, the daughter of a retired NBA player, says she is “a bit competitive” but still friendly. “I’m so freaking extra. Please ‘Love Island,’ give me a man!” she shouts.
Beatriz Hatz
Current city: San Diego, California
Age: 25
Occupation: Professional athlete
Instagram: bhatz_track
Beatriz was born with a “rare birth defect,” she says. But that hasn’t stopped her from becoming a Paralympian. Her type on paper? “Tall, dark and handsome.”
Bryce Dettloff
Current city: Los Angeles, California
Age: 29
Occupation: DJ
Instagram: brycealakai

Bryce says he loves music but he also works as a handyman. “I do electrical work on garbage trucks sometimes. It’s literally so disgusting but it’s like, you’ve got to do what you’ve got to do,” he says.
KC Chandler
Current city: Fresno, California
Age: 23
Occupation: Nursing assistant
Instagram: kc.thagreat
KC is a nursing assistant who says “women love men in healthcare.” He adds, “If you’re man and you have scrubs, your muscles showing a little bit, they are going for that every time.”
Kenzie Annis
Current city: Kennesaw, Georgia
Age: 24
Occupation: Nursing school graduate
Instagram: kenzieannis

Kenzie received her degree from nursing school two days before heading to the villa. “I want a huge family, five to 10 kids. I know that’s kind of crazy!” she says, adding that she would also like to adopt.
Melanie Moreno
Current city: Los Angeles, California
Age: 24
Occupation: Bikini store manager
Instagram: melanieelisa
Melanie is ready to bring her Dominican flare into the villa. Currently, she says the bikinis in the “damaged bin” of her store describe her love life. Hopefully things turn around this summer.
Sean Reifel
Current city: Easton, Pennsylvania
Age: 29
Occupation: Police officer
Instagram: parm3_sean

Sean started off in a seemingly strong couple with Beatriz. But after Beatriz was wooed by Gabriel, one of the first bombshells of the season, her connection with Sean fizzled. He had a budding relationship with Kenzie, but she was chosen by Corbin during the first recoupling of the season. As the last man standing, Sean became the first islander of the season to be dumped. He said he was grateful for the “once in a lifetime” experience and that he enjoyed getting to know everyone.
Sincere Rhea
Current city: Cape May, New Jersey
Age: 25
Occupation: Athlete
Instagram: sincere.rhea
Sincere is a big fan of his name and says it helps him with pickup lines. “I like to be cheesy. I often end my emails, like, ‘Sincerely Sincere.’” He also says he likes the chase.
Trinity Tatum
Current city: Newport News, Virginia
Age: 22
Occupation: Model
Instagram: soootrini
Trinity is from a small town where she says that dating a second cousin could be a possibility. “So, I just want a fresh start,” she shares. “I love a respectful man.”
Vasana Montgomery
Current city: Beaverton, Oregon
Age: 25
Occupation: Business owner
Instagram: vasana_vibes

After her casting was announced, videos began circulating online of her appearing to say a racial slur on camera, and she was removed from the cast by May 29. She did not respond to NBC News’ request for comment.
Peacock confirmed to NBC News that Montgomery will no longer be on the show. (Peacock is owned by NBCUniversal, the parent company of TODAY.com.)
Zach Georgiou
Current city: Birmingham, England
Age: 26
Occupation: Digital creator
Instagram: zachgeorgio

Zach is the brother of Charlie Georgiou, who was a bombshell in Season 7. Zach says he is here to see if he “can do better” during his time on the show. He has a cat named Lolo and is looking for someone who is a cat person.
Meet the ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Bombshells
Gabriel Vasconcelos
Current city: Miami, Florida
Age: 26
Occupation: Model
Instagram: gvasconcelosv
Gabriel wants to bring the “Brazilian fire” to Fiji. He speaks three languages: Portuguese, Spanish and English. But, he adds that making out is his love language.
Kayda Reese Bosse
Current city: Manchester, New Hampshire
Age: 22
Occupation: Server
Instagram: kaydabosse

Kayda, along with Gabriel, entered the villa as a bombshell in Episode 1. “I’m 10 out of 10, these men are gonna fall to their knees for me,” she said in a voiceover as she arrived. “I’m in my player mode. These other girls should definitely watch out. I’m gonna step on toes if I have to.”
Corbin Mims
Current city: Miami, Florida
Age: 22
Occupation: Business owner
Instagram: corbiskii

Corbin’s grand entrance came in the first challenge of the season. During their turn, each girl entered a private room and found Corbin waiting for them before they shared a steamy, secret kiss. title: Matt Cornett Shares the Advice He Got From Old Roommate, ‘The Summer I Turned Pretty’s’ Gavin Casalegno
summary: When Matt Cornett booked Prime Video’s “Every Year After,” he knew just who to call: his old roommate and “The Summer I Turned Pretty” star Gavin Casalegno.
story: When Matt Cornett booked the lead role in Prime Video’s “Every Year After,” he knew just who to call: his old roommate.

“Every Year After” is the latest Prime Video book-to-screen adaptation. The romance follows childhood friends Sam Florek (Cornett) and Percy Fraser (Sadie Soverall) as they spent their summers together in Barry’s Bay, Ontario. The eight-episode series is adapted from the 2022 bestselling novel by Carley Fortune, titled “Every Summer After.”

While appearing on TODAY June 10 alongside Soverall, Cornett shared that he used to room with actor Gavin Casalegno, known for his role as Jeremiah Fisher in “The Summer I Turned Pretty” — another Prime Video book-to-screen adaptation.
“Gavin and I have been friends for quite a long time,” Cornett said on TODAY June 10. “We lived together before he got ‘The Summer I Turned Pretty.’
“When I told him I was doing this show, we actually were at lunch one day,” Cornett added. “I was like, ‘Yeah, I’m doing this next Amazon book adaptation, summer show.’”
Casalegno immediately offered some advice.
“He was like, ‘Get ready, the book fans are incredible, and they are intense,’” Cornett recalled.
He added that he loves the fan dedication.
“They know their books, and I think they know Sam better than me, which is kind of scary sometimes,” he said.
“Means you are doing it right,” co-host Craig Melvin chimed in.

“Every Year After” was given a series order in 2024, and the cast was announced in July 2025. Alongside Fortune, the series is backed by showrunner and executive producer Amy B. Harris, who came into the project with two previous book-to-screen experiences on her resume: “The Carrie Diaries and “Sex and the City.”
All eight episodes are available to stream — or, more likely, binge —  now on Prime Video.

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Season 8 Power Rankings: Week 1’s Winners, Losers and Surprises
summary: TODAY.com’s “Love Island USA” Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin discuss the first fan vote of the season.
story: In TODAY.com’s new weekly “Love Island USA” column, content creators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin break down the latest group of Season 8 episodes, sharing their thoughts on the new bombshells, couplings and dumping. Check back here over the next six weeks to read the duo’s hot takes and follow @tessdhiggins and @sallydarrgriffin on their socials to keep up with them throughout the week.
It has been a messy few days in the “Love Island USA” villa.
Season 8 of the Peacock reality dating series has already delivered jaw-dropping challenges, six bold bombshells and one emotional elimination in just a week.

In Episode 5, Sean became the first islander to be dumped from the villa. Then, Episode 6 featured a new challenge that involved a giant bed, blindfolds, handcuffs, paint and three bombshells. The arrival of Caleb, Jen and Sol brought tests for multiple couples, including one pair that TODAY.com’s “Love Island USA” Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr both labeled as their “Best Couple” after the premiere.
By the end of Episode 7 the following couples remained in the villa…
Trinity and Bryce
Trinity and Bryce
Melanie and Sincere
Melanie and Sincere
Kenzie and Corbin
Kenzie and Corbin
Aniya and KC
Aniya and KC
Kayda and Zach
Kayda and Zach
Beatriz and Gabriel
Beatriz and Gabriel

But those pairs might not last for long as the episode concluded with narrator Iain Stirling announcing the first fan vote of the season. Viewers can vote via the app on the islander they want Caleb, Jen and Sol to couple up with. The results will be shown on the next episode, out Thursday, June 11.
How do Sally Darr and Tess think the vote will play out?
Sally Darr says Melanie, Corbin and Bea could find themselves single and vulnerable, and she isn’t sure who would be dumped between the three of them.

“There’s kind of two schools of thought with this,” she explains. “Do you keep the people who are building connections or do you keep the people who need more time? In previous years, specifically in the last season, I feel like the islanders were always keeping people who “needed more time” or who just got here.

As for Tess, she says Melanie’s spot in the villa could be in jeopardy. “But I don’t think that they’re going to want to get rid of Melanie so soon, because that would leave kind of an empty drama gap,” she adds.
Read on for more of their thoughts about the latest episodes, including the unraveling of one fan-favorite pair, below.
Best Moment

Tess: “I loved when we got to see Bryce giving Trinity the tea on how Melanie was going back and forth between kissing Corbin and Sincere. It really just highlighted their playful dynamic. I think it was like a really memeable moment, too, just to see them laughing and going back and forth gossiping about what was going on.”

Sally Darr: “Melanie denying that she knew who she was kissing in the paint challenge was cracking me up because she definitely knew. Specifically when KC was talking to her and he was saying, ‘Melanie, you know I love you. Let’s be honest here.’ And she just continued to deny it. Bryce and Trinity were also joking in their bed. Trinity was saying, ‘You know she was blindfolded. Maybe she genuinely didn’t know.’ Bryce was saying ‘Trinity, who are we kidding here?’ They were giggling and laughing. I just thought that her continuing to deny it was so funny. Then on top of that, Sincere kissing Sol in the “Say Less” area, and later the Speakeasy area, but we’ll get to that. Sincere kissing Sol was a really pointed response to how Melanie has been moving and I’m here for the drama. You know that whole situation is just giving us really good TV.”
Biggest Surprise

Tess: “Sol and Sincere’s kiss because I didn’t expect him to go for it right away, and the fact that Melanie almost walked in on them was timed out so perfectly for maximum impact. I really think it’s going to impact the way that the audience votes, being left with that as the last thing we see at the end of the episode.”

Sally Darr: “I would say Sincere not telling Melanie that he kissed Sol twice. He told her that he kissed Sol in the Speakeasy area, but he did not tell her that he kissed her earlier in the “Say Less” area. I just think that that could come back to hurt him, not giving her the full truth. We know that the ‘Love Island’ producers can use that kind of stuff as ammo on something like “Movie Night.” I think it’s fine that they kissed but I think we’ve gotten to know Melanie. I don’t think she’s going to be happy with not having the full truth. I think that was really naive of him.”
Best Couple

Tess: “Aniya and KC. I really like how they’re being portrayed so far. We’re seeing a lot of depth to their connection, especially in that conversation they had on the dock in this episode, where Aniya was expressing how she was upset about what happened during the challenge. (It) kind of felt like he understood what she meant and was going to take accountability and try to right the wrongs.”

Sally Darr: “Bryce and Trinity are cracking me up. I don’t know if it’s just a friendship thing, if it’s going to grow into more, but they seem to have good banter. They’re giggling, they’re having fun. I also love KC and Aniya. I am a tiny bit worried that KC’s head is going to turn because he is kind of interested in Sol. But he did reassure Aniya so I hope that they’re good. I hope that they stay together. I think that they’re really sweet.”
Biggest Winner

Tess: “Trinity and Bryce as a couple, just because they have so little drama and are just having fun right now. Everyone else has conflict in their couple to some degree, which makes Trinity and Bryce’s scenes more easy to watch and more fun.”

Sally Darr: “All of the bombshells have the advantage right now. I would say that Sol specifically is crushing it. She’s really shaken things up and I think that she’s showing the audience that she isn’t just going to go for the easy option. She kissed Gabriel and she said that she wasn’t interested, so she’s not just going to take attention wherever she can get it. She’s coming across as really genuine and she’s really interested in Sincere. So she’s kind of breaking up a couple that’s been together since day one. If she’s going to be hanging out with someone and kissing someone, it’s going to be because she’s actually into it. I think she’s kind of building a trust with the audience in a way. She’s the winner tonight.”
At Risk of Being Dumped

Tess: “Melanie. I think she’s the most vulnerable. Because if the votes go the way I think they’re going to go, I think Caleb will be paired with Kenzie, Jen with Corbin and Sol with Sincere. But I don’t think that they’re going to want to get rid of Melanie so soon, because that would leave kind of an empty drama gap. I feel like they’ll bring someone in for her soon, because she’s really bringing it when it comes to creating that conflict between her and Sincere.”

Sally Darr: “The question on the ‘Love Island’ app tonight is: ‘Who do you want to pair these bombshells up with?’ We have Sol, Caleb and Jen. I think the public will pair Sol with Sincere, Caleb with Kenzie, Jen with Gabriel. Potentially Jen with Zach, but I think they’ll do Gabriel. Based off of that, the people who are at risk are the current partners of those people: Melanie, Corbin and Bea. Those are the three Islanders at risk. I don’t know what the producers are going to do with this. My guess is they’re going to make the current islanders vote between these three people who they want to go home. I don’t know what the islanders are going to do. Are they going to send home Melanie, Bea or Corbin? There’s kind of two schools of thought with this. Do you keep the people who are building connections or do you keep the people who need more time? In previous years, specifically in the last season, I feel like the islanders were always keeping people who ‘needed more time’ or who just got here. That makes me think that they’re going to keep Corbin. I personally think you keep the people who have made connections. Honestly, none of them have a really strong connection. Currently, it’s kind of a toss-up. Corbin just got there. Bea does have a thing with Gabriel, but Gabriel’s kind of had his head turned a little bit. He’s kissing everyone. Melanie likes Sincere, but she’s kind of been moving in a weird way. I think Melanie’s kind of divisive in the villa, so maybe there’s some people that aren’t going to opt to keep her. Who’s to say? It’s going to be a toss-up if that’s what the producers do. But maybe the producers won’t make them pick, so we’ll see.”

title: Carley Fortune Shares the Parts of Her That Inspired the 2 ‘Every Year After’ Leads
summary: Carley Fortune speaks about the adaptation of her debut novel Every Summer After into the book Every Year After.
story: Carley Fortune is still her 10 year old self. And 13. And 16.

It’s an epiphany that came about years ago, as she re-read her journals from growing up in Barry’s Bay in Canada. A lake town — population, 1,084 — Fortune grew up down a dirt road in the woods. Her summers were spent in the waters of Kamaniskeg Lake, reading books on her dock and working at her parents’ restaurant.
On her street, three of the houses, like Fortune’s, were lived in year-round. The rest were used by cottagers, people who visited the lake with the seasons.

The main character of Fortune’s debut novel, “Every Summer After,” is one of those cottagers. Persephone Fraser spends her summers in Barry’s Bay and the rest of the year in Toronto. At 13 years old, she meets Sam Florek, who lives full time at the lake.

“Sam has exactly how I grew up,” Fortune tells TODAY.com in an interview. “He grows up on the lake in Barry’s Bay. His parents have a restaurant. He is very ambitious, and he really wants to build a life outside of his small town.”
But it’s Percy who’s “infused with so much of my insecurity and anxiety,” she says.

“Every Summer After” published in May 2022. Now it’s headed to the small screen, with an eight-episode series adaptation, retitled “Every Year After,” which dropped on Prime Video June 10. As the story comes to life before Fortune’s eyes, she’s thinking of the girl on the lake who was so desperately “longing for someone to really understand” her.

title: ‘Love Island’ Fans Can Experience the Villa at 30 Rock for 3 Days this June
summary: The Love Island villa is coming to Rockefeller Plaza from June 22 to June 24. Here’s how to find out.
story: Attention “Love Island USA” fans, the villa is heading to the states.

If you’ve ever wanted to know what it is like to get ready in the glam room for a night filled with drama and dancing or to deliver a recoupling speech at the fire pit, now is your chance. Peacock will bring iconic parts of the villa to New York City for a special three-day immersive fan event.

The pop-up will be held at Rockefeller Center and allow fans to experience the day-to-day life of an islander. Fans who visit the activation, which will be open from Monday, June 22 to Wednesday, June 24, will receive themed giveaways. They will also be able to snap pictures, create their own must-see bombshell moment and enjoy more interactive experiences.

The activation space will include build outs of the girls’ glam room, fire pit and more nods to locations around the villa. In the glam room, fans can stop by Maybelline’s shade match station and snag some beauty must-haves. At the fire pit set up, there will be a claw machine filled with CeraVe products. The skincare company will be giving out Blemish Barrier patches to fans, as well.
Register for The Villa at Rockefeller Center!
Sign up here.
Sign up here.
Additionally, fans can grab a customized bag charm that reads “Bombshell” or “Coupled Up” to let everyone know their status.
The pop-up, which is a partnership with CeraVe and Maybelline New York, will be open from 12 p.m. to 7 p.m. ET daily.

The event comes as fans are already invested in the “Love Island USA” Season 8 couples after just one week. So far, viewers have watched multiple bombshells make their grand entrances and one original islander get dumped during the first recoupling of the season. Fans can continue to follow along with the islanders as new episodes of the reality dating show arrive every day, except Wednesdays, at 9 p.m. ET / 6 p.m. PT on Peacock.

title: Jenna and Sheinelle Answer 2 Burning Questions from Fans, And They’re Looking for More
summary: Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones are giving viewers a look at the inner workings of their show and friendship in a new segment called “Ask Away.”The TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle co-hosts debuted the segment June 9 and shared two questions from fans of the show, which were submitted on Instagram.
story: Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones are giving viewers a look at the inner workings of their show and friendship in a new segment called “Ask Away.”
The TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle co-hosts debuted the segment June 9 and shared two questions from fans of the show, which were submitted on Instagram.
Up first, they answered the following query: “Do you have a favorite memory together?”
Sheinelle went first and recalled a time when the TODAY crew hung out on a rooftop in New York City for the whole day.
“About how many years ago was that?” she asked Jenna.
“Three or four or five,” Jenna replied.
“It started around, say, noon. Would you like to tell America when it ended?” Sheinelle said.
“Nine p.m., right?” Jenna asked.
Sheinelle continued, trying to remember if Dylan Dreyer had “chipped her tooth” at the get together. Then, she realized she’d actually “skinned her knee.”
“We haven’t done a fun lunch in a long time. But we like to do that. It’s really fun,” Sheinelle said.
Next, it was Jenna’s turn to share her favorite memory with her co-host, which occurred during one of many Olympics games TODAY has covered over the years.
“We were kind of on standby, so we didn’t do much. And we spent the entire time, or I did, learning TikTok dances with Sheinelle,” she said.
“It was a good time,” Sheinelle agreed.
“It was fun. It’s what the kids do, bro,” Jenna joked.
For their second and final question of the day, the co-hosts offered insight into their on-air wardrobe while answering the question, “Do you choose your own clothes?”
While the co-hosts often do pick out their own outfits, Sheinelle said she got a bit of help this morning.
“I was going to do something Knicks-related, and then they didn’t win, so I was like, ‘OK, well, let me keep the top,’” she said of her orange blouse.
Sheinelle then explained that one of her colleagues suggested she wear a pair of brown pants.
“I think it looks chic,” Jenna said.
“I’ve never worn orange and brown,” Sheinelle said.
Jenna, who was wearing a khaki dress, then suggested that she and Sheinelle “both kind of look like we’re going on some sort of safari.”
Keep an eye on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle’s Instagram for another chance to submit questions.

title: ‘Glass Half Full’: Rich Paul Opens up About Moving from Friends to More with Adele
summary: Rich Paul tells Craig Melvin’s “Glass Half Full” how he met Adele and they wound up going from friends to a couple.
story: Rich Paul represents some of the biggest names in sports, but it’s how he landed a relationship with one of the biggest names in music that he’s talking about now.
The Klutch Sports Group founder, agent and entrepreneur opened up about meeting longtime partner Adele on the June 9 episode of Craig Melvin’s “Glass Half Full” podcast.
“How did you meet Adele?” Craig asked.

“Through a friend. I had known her for some time,” Paul said. “You know, just when you’re in these circles, man, you’re in these circles. But I never tried to get fresh with people that’s in comfortable circles because they always have to deal with that, so that was never my thing. It was really something that happened very organically, really.”
Paul, who represents stars like LeBron James, Anthony Davis, A’ja Wilson and Tyrese Maxey, said he wasn’t too familiar with her music before they met.

“Obviously, you can’t help but to hear the monster hits,” he said when asked if he had previously listed to Adele’s music. “But, no, not exactly. But obviously, you know, I’m pretty on the pulse of a lot of different things, and so, you know, some of the songs, the big songs, but I didn’t know all of the joints that I know now.”
Pressed for more details on the origins of their relationship, Paul said they continued to run into each other.
“We’re all in the same circles, and, you know, we’re hanging or whatnot,” he said. “We just used to always see each other, laugh and joke. It was just cordial, really, just cordial.”
“Until?” Craig said.
“Until it became not so cordial. Until I became a person of interest,” he joked, prompting Craig to laugh.
“A suspect,” quipped Craig.
Paul also may be with one of the world’s biggest pop stars, but he said he doesn’t shy away from showing off his pipes.
“I’m the best singer in the house,” he joked.

Adele and Paul started dating in 2021 and have kept their relationship relatively private. The two sparked engagement rumors back in 2024 after Adele responded to a fan’s poster at her concert with, “‘Will you marry me?’ I can’t marry you because I’m already getting married, so I can’t.”
Watch Craig’s full conversation with Paul for “Glass Half Full” on TODAY’s YouTube channel or listen wherever you get your podcasts.

title: Elon Musk under fire for stoking anti-immigrant riots in Belfast
summary: LONDON — The condemnation over anti-immigrant riots in Northern Ireland was being matched by another growing outrage in Britain on Thursday: that the world’s richest person was inciting the violence
story: LONDON — The condemnation over anti-immigrant riots in Northern Ireland was being matched by another growing outrage in Britain on Thursday: that the world’s richest person was inciting the violence.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

A second night of unrest on the streets of Belfast saw masked men once again torch houses and vehicles in a hunt for anyone they believed to be an immigrant. A government minister labeled the violence “racist thuggery.”

The rioters say they are motivated by the stabbing of a local man, Stephen Ogilvie, who remains in a coma after losing one eye in the knife attack that also caused deep wounds to his face and neck. Hadi Alodid, a 30-year-old Sudanese man, has been arrested and charged with attempted murder, but did not enter a plea when he appeared in court Wednesday.
Graphic video of the attack spread quickly and widely on social media. Police have not revealed a motive for the stabbing attack but said it’s not believed to be terrorism.
Ogilvie’s family have implored people not to use the attack for political gain or violence, while stressing the “valuable contribution” that migrants make to society.

title: The inflation surge is hitting businesses, too
summary: Wholesale inflation surged to its highest level since late 2022, as fuel prices pushed higher by the war with Iran ripple across the economy.
story: Business inflation in May surged to its highest level since late 2022, as high fuel prices triggered by the war with Iran ripple across the U.S. economy.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
New data released Thursday showed that from April to May, the producer price index saw a 1.1% month-over-month rise.
Even more notably, it rose to 6.5% from the same period a year ago.
The year-over-year reading was “the largest 12-month rise since moving up 7.4% in November 2022,” the Bureau of Labor Statistics said in a statement.
The data comes one day after the consumer price index showed that overall inflation rose in May to its highest level since early 2023.
Core PPI, which excludes food and energy costs, rose to 4.9% annually.
The jump in producer prices surprised economists and drew renewed attention to President Donald Trump’s remark Wednesday at the White House where he said, “I love the inflation.”
“When the war is over, it’s coming down, it’s going to come down like a rock,” Trump added.
The president later claimed that his words had been taken out of context, telling the New York Post, “The numbers are much lower than anticipated.”
But neither Wednesday’s consumer price index nor Thursday’s business inflation report were lower than expected.
The direct line from higher gas prices to higher inflation was stark: BLS said that 80% of the surge in the overall PPI figure Thursday was attributable to surging energy costs.
Taken together, the PPI and CPI readings reveal an economy under ever more stress from oil prices that remain 40% higher since the United States and Israel launched the Iran war Feb. 28.
So far this year, oil prices have climbed about 60%.
E.J. Antoni, Trump’s former nominee to lead BLS, described the business inflation report Thursday as “eye-watering.”
“This is getting really ugly,” he wrote in a post on X.
Thursday’s data will also likely factor into the a decision by the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates on hold for now.

Still, futures market traders are currently projecting that the central bank will have to hike rates in order to tame inflation by December, with a 60% chance that a hike could happen as soon as October.
“The Fed will be hard pressed to look through the firming in inflation,” said Stephen Juneau, U.S. economist at Bank of America.
Central banks typically discount or “look through” energy inflation over short periods of time until higher prices spill into other sectors of the economy.
Also contributing to higher producer prices was a sharp 4.8% rise in portfolio management fees, which also came alongside a seemingly unstoppable rise in stocks.
Earlier Thursday, another central bank, the European Central Bank, hiked interest rates. ECB president Christine Lagarde said that inflation was not projected to return to 2% until late 2027.

title: Pope Leo blesses Barcelona’s towering architectural masterpiece
summary: Pope Leo XIV offered his formal blessing to the highest spire of the world’s tallest church, when he holds Mass at the Sagrada Família in Barcelona.
story: BARCELONA, Spain — Pope Leo XIV offered his formal blessing Wednesday to the highest spire of what is now the world’s tallest church, holding Mass at the iconic Sagrada Família in Barcelona.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The event, the highlight of a weeklong trip to Spain for Leo, comes 144 years after construction on the unfinished modernist basilica began and 100 years since the death of its famous architect, Antoni Gaudí.

Standing 566 feet high and crowned with a five-story ceramic cross, the central Tower of Jesus Christ is the highest of 18 adorning the Sagrada Família, a bucket-list item for most travelers to the city in Spain’s northeastern Catalonia region.

Leo’s visit commemorates the legacy of Gaudí, whose radical, modernist designs were mocked in his lifetime but have subsequently been celebrated. Gaudí took over the construction of Sagrada Família in 1883, a year after the first cornerstone was laid during the pontificate of Leo’s namesake, Pope Leo XIII.
“We are all the living stones of this edifice,” Leo said from the altar of the basilica.

title: Why a luxury resort plan by Trump’s son-in-law sparked mass protests in Albania
summary: President Donald Trump’s son-in-law has a formidable new opponent: a pink flamingo
story: President Donald Trump’s son-in-law has a formidable new opponent: a pink flamingo.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
The protected bird has emerged as the symbol of a surging protest movement against a Jared Kushner-backed plan for a luxury resort on the picturesque Adriatic coast.

The $1.6 billion project has drawn thousands of protesters — some wielding inflatable flamingos — to the streets of Albania’s capital over fears the planned tourist complex threatens an ecologically sensitive area that is home to pristine beaches and a variety of protected species.

Large protests in Tirana this week saw chants of “Albania is not for sale,” as demonstrators clashed with police, who responded with water cannons. Protesters gathered Thursday outside Prime Minister Edi Rama’s residence chanting “revolution” and calling for his resignation.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 schedule guide: Where to watch, time, streaming and more

Where can you watch the World Cup in the U.S.?
World Cup coverage in the U.S. can be found on Fox and FS1 for English speakers. Spanish-language broadcasts will be hosted on Telemundo and Universo.
Where can you stream the World Cup?

Matches generally start between 12 p.m. and 12 a.m. ET. The games span multiple time zones.
